summary: Read-only audit of the user's DinoBrain OS completion target and work-package checklist. Verified the governing completion documents, current project state, package verifier registry, live Codex pre-response evidence, full verify:goal result, health rollup, and direct MCP parity status. npm was absent from PATH, so verification used DinoBrain portable Node. After refreshing full-memory audit state, npm run verify:goal passed. Remaining strict caveat: client_mcp_direct_status still reports needs_recheck for Codex and Claude exact single-name direct MCP canary proof, so P0-04 should not be marked fully complete under the user's stricter checklist even though verify:goal is green.
decisions: Treat the listed Final Target / Completion Bar / P0-P2 plan as directionally correct and aligned with docs/OS_COMPLETION_CONDITIONS.md.
Use npm run verify:goal as the current global acceptance gate, but do not let it erase the stricter P0-04 direct-client MCP parity caveat.
Current live Codex hook proof exists for this prompt: codex_prompt_submitted and codex_preflight_completed were observed with six context paths.
Direct MCP parity remains needs_recheck until exact single-name client canary proof is recorded for Codex and Claude.
next_steps: Implement or run the direct client MCP canary proof writer so client_mcp_direct_status can move from needs_recheck to verified for Codex and Claude where installed.
Consider wiring health_status/client_mcp_direct_status into verify:goal if P0-04 is intended to be a hard global completion blocker.
Commit or intentionally leave the generated data-vault verification artifacts according to the user's next sync decision.
